current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a proxy `mixture_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether soft expert routing can materially beat the current 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory.
Run a proxy `jumprelu` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether learnable thresholding can materially improve reconstruction quality.
Run a proxy `gated` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether decoupling selection and magnitude can materially improve reconstruction quality.

action.hypothesis: `factorized_topk` missed badly at K=128, so the next plausible quality-focused family is a softly routed multi-expert encoder; `mixture_topk` may improve reconstruction by adding conditional encoder capacity without the hard routing bottlenecks of grouped variants.
action.summary: Run a proxy `mixture_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether soft expert routing can materially beat the current 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory.
action.self_review: This is another first-pass architecture probe, so it may still be far from competitive or may need family-specific tuning before it shows value. If it is only slightly better than `factorized_topk` but still far behind TopK, that is not enough to justify promotion.
notes_to_memory: `factorized_topk` prototype quality was poor at K=128 proxy (FVU 0.6381136849522591) despite normal runtime, so simple nonlinear hidden mixing does not currently look like a path to the aggressive target.
Keep architecture exploration focused on families with qualitatively different encoder behavior rather than continuing local retuning of the weak `factorized_topk` prototype.
next_hypotheses: If `mixture_topk` shows a clear proxy quality gain over plain TopK at K=128, promote it and then tune family-specific capacity.
If `mixture_topk` is also clearly worse than TopK, shift the next architecture probe toward a different sparse activation family such as `jumprelu` or a new routed/multi-branch design.
If `mixture_topk` is promising but slow, follow with a targeted performance pass instead of discarding the family immediately.

action.hypothesis: Both encoder-capacity families tried so far are far behind the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next coherent probe is a different sparse activation mechanism rather than more mixing capacity; `jumprelu` may improve feature selection quality by learning per-latent activation thresholds.
action.summary: Run a proxy `jumprelu` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether learnable thresholding can materially improve reconstruction quality.
action.self_review: This is still a first-pass family probe, and the default threshold/bandwidth settings may be poorly tuned. If the result is weak, that may mean either the family is unpromising here or that it needs a stabilization pass before a real comparison.
notes_to_memory: `mixture_topk` improved over `factorized_topk` but remained far behind the mainline K=128 TopK anchor, so extra encoder mixture capacity alone is not closing the quality gap.
Continue exploring qualitatively different architecture families at fixed K=128 until one shows a real path below the 0.211975 anchor.
next_hypotheses: If `jumprelu` is competitive in proxy, follow with a stabilization round on its threshold and bandwidth parameters before promotion.
If `jumprelu` is also clearly worse than TopK, move to another qualitatively different family such as `gated` or a new multi-branch sparse encoder.
If `jumprelu` is quality-promising but unstable, keep the family incubating and tune its activation hyperparameters rather than discarding it immediately.

action.hypothesis: The explored nonlinear, mixture, and thresholded families are all well behind the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next clean probe is `gated`, which changes selection dynamics by separating gate and magnitude branches and could improve active-feature choice without requiring a brand-new codepath.
action.summary: Run a proxy `gated` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether decoupling selection and magnitude can materially improve reconstruction quality.
action.self_review: This is still a first-pass family check, and `gated` may simply inherit the same quality ceiling as plain TopK while adding optimization complexity. If it lands near the other failed prototypes, that strengthens the case for returning to TopK-focused quality work or implementing a genuinely new family.
notes_to_memory: `jumprelu` at default hyperparameters matched the weak proxy band of other alternative families and did not show a plausible path below the K=128 TopK anchor.

The architecture search so far suggests that modest encoder expressivity changes are not enough; remaining probes should either be qualitatively different or push back toward stronger TopK-family quality interventions.
next_hypotheses: If `gated` is competitive in proxy, promote it or tune its optimization settings before broader conclusions.

If `gated` is also clearly worse than TopK, stop cycling through near-neighbor families and either try a stronger TopK-quality regularization pass or implement a genuinely new routed/multi-branch family.
If `gated` is quality-promising but slower, keep it incubating and follow with a targeted performance pass.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The new K=128 TopK+Adam anchor at FVU 0.211975 is strong enough to stop local optimizer tinkering for now; a more expressive nonlinear encoder is the next plausible path toward a materially lower FVU, so `factorized_topk` should be tested at the same K=128 anchor settings.
next_steps: If `factorized_topk` shows materially better proxy FVU at K=128, promote it and then tune its encoder width or related family-specific capacity.
If `factorized_topk` is neutral or worse, try another expressive encoder family such as `mixture_topk` rather than returning immediately to smaller-K search.
If the new family is quality-promising but noticeably slower, treat that as a performance-fix follow-up instead of an immediate architectural rejection.

mixture_topk.design_hypothesis: `factorized_topk` missed badly at K=128, so the next plausible quality-focused family is a softly routed multi-expert encoder; `mixture_topk` may improve reconstruction by adding conditional encoder capacity without the hard routing bottlenecks of grouped variants.
next_steps: If `mixture_topk` shows a clear proxy quality gain over plain TopK at K=128, promote it and then tune family-specific capacity.
If `mixture_topk` is also clearly worse than TopK, shift the next architecture probe toward a different sparse activation family such as `jumprelu` or a new routed/multi-branch design.
If `mixture_topk` is promising but slow, follow with a targeted performance pass instead of discarding the family immediately.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: Both encoder-capacity families tried so far are far behind the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next coherent probe is a different sparse activation mechanism rather than more mixing capacity; `jumprelu` may improve feature selection quality by learning per-latent activation thresholds.
next_steps: If `jumprelu` is competitive in proxy, follow with a stabilization round on its threshold and bandwidth parameters before promotion.
If `jumprelu` is also clearly worse than TopK, move to another qualitatively different family such as `gated` or a new multi-branch sparse encoder.
If `jumprelu` is quality-promising but unstable, keep the family incubating and tune its activation hyperparameters rather than discarding it immediately.

gated.design_hypothesis: The explored nonlinear, mixture, and thresholded families are all well behind the K=128 TopK anchor, so the next clean probe is `gated`, which changes selection dynamics by separating gate and magnitude branches and could improve active-feature choice without requiring a brand-new codepath.
next_steps: If `gated` is competitive in proxy, promote it or tune its optimization settings before broader conclusions.

If `gated` is also clearly worse than TopK, stop cycling through near-neighbor families and either try a stronger TopK-quality regularization pass or implement a genuinely new routed/multi-branch family.
If `gated` is quality-promising but slower, keep it incubating and follow with a targeted performance pass.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: The first-step timeout is caused by serialized DDP startup for local artifacts rather than by the K=128 TopK/Adam configuration itself; allowing both ranks to load the local model and dataset in parallel should get the proxy run to first metrics. -> keep (fvu=0.2119751125574112, observed_fvu=0.220770, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)
next_move_guidance: If `gated` is competitive in proxy, promote it or tune its optimization settings before broader conclusions.

If `gated` is also clearly worse than TopK, stop cycling through near-neighbor families and either try a stronger TopK-quality regularization pass or implement a genuinely new routed/multi-branch family.
